signals.headline: Markets pricing 97-99% chance of no Fed rate change at the April 28-29 meeting, driven by 3
signals.raw: Markets pricing 97-99% chance of no Fed rate change at the April 28-29 meeting, driven by 3.3% annual CPI inflation (up from prior months) and Iran-related oil shocks, ruling out cuts or hikes for now.[1][2]

signals.headline: March 17-18 FOMC minutes (released ~April 8) show "some" officials open to rate hikes if inflation persists, with PCE at
signals.raw: March 17-18 FOMC minutes (released ~April 8) show "some" officials open to rate hikes if inflation persists, with PCE at 2.8% total/3.1% core in January (est. 2.8%/3.0% February); no new actions taken.[5][6][7]

signals.headline: March FOMC held rates at 3
signals.raw: March FOMC held rates at 3.5-3.75%, projecting one 2026 cut but higher inflation (PCE 2.7%) amid energy risks; Kevin Warsh noted as potential Powell successor facing divisions.[4][6]

signals.headline: No mentions of South Korea or new confirmations/announcements post-March; upcoming March CPI (forecast 3
signals.raw: No mentions of South Korea or new confirmations/announcements post-March; upcoming March CPI (forecast 3.4% annual) due soon but not yet released.[5]
